A tessera hospitalis recording xenia between Imulch and Lyson
Four lines of Greek letters, with a consistent left margin, starting at the top of the rear (smooth) side of the tessera, with small vacat below.

Small, slightly irregular letters without serifs. Lunate epsilon, sigma and omega; mu formed by four, equal slanting strokes; omicron generally slightly smaller than other letters; beta is closed, but with separated loops; rho is closed; xsi in form of a Z with horizontal cross bar; kappa has short equal length arms.
2nd century BCE, or possibly 1st century BCE (the letter forms look more plausibly second than 1st century)
Paciaudi, 1751, Diatribe qua Graeci anaglyphi interpretatio traditur
Castelli, 1753, Storia di Alesa, antica città di Sicilia
Castelli, 1762, Le antiche iscrizioni di Palermo

Lilibeo e il Mare. Il museo archeologico regionale di Marsala, 2024

1Ιμυλχ Ιμιλχωνος
2Ινιβαλος Χλωρὸς ξενίαν
3ἐποήσατο πρὸς Λύσων
4Διογνήτου καὶ τῶν ἐγγόνων
Lilybaeum honours Diognetos Megas as euergetes

Regular and neat letters, thinly incised, all of equal height, and slightly variable width, without serifs. Alpha with broken bar, beta with closed loops (upper slightly smaller), Mu has full length slanting strokes; omicron full size; sigma has horizontal top and bottom, middle strokes taking the full width; rhomboid omega.
2nd or 1st century BCE (as late as early Augustan acc. to Wilson and Manni Piraino; but Manni Piraino also suggests 2nd century BCE). The form of the letters might imply an earlier date in this range
Manni Piraino, 1963, Due Iscrizioni inedite di Marsala

Pellegrini describes the form of the letters as similar to that  and ; he also notes that they have been painted in red, although he appears to imply that this was done in modern times, with the praecnomen and the interpunct of line 9 remaining unpainted.
Dated to the period of Diocletian or Constantine, but probably prior to 314 CE, on the basis of the office and titles

painted letters, simple in style without serifs and not perfectly following a horizontal line. Alpha has straight bar; theta is circular with a central point; M is very broad, all strokes diagonal, the mid-point not fully down to the lower line; sigma is lunate.
Gabrici, 1929, Stele sepolcrali di Lilibeo a forma di Heroon
Bisi, 1970, Influenze italiote e siceliote sull'arte tardo-punica. Le stele funerarie di Lilibeo
Vento, 2000, Le stele dipinte di Lilibeo

1Μαρία ἥρυς
2ἀγαθά
1Δε...ς ἥρως ἀγαθός

Relatively broad brushstrokes with some fluidity. Alpha is broad with straight bar; beta has distinct closed eyes, somewhat angular; eta is broad with outward turning lower strokes; theta is ovid with horizontal bar; mu is broad with vertical uprights, and the middle strokes only descending half-way; omicron is sometimes slightly smaller and above the line; sigma is lunate; omega is lunate.

1Μάρχος Σημβρόνις
2ἥρως ἀγαθός

2ἀγαθός

[⁦ - - - - - - - - - - ⁩]
Text over two lines in the upper central part of the rear wall of the niche on the front of the aedicula; text sits below a set of decorative symbols and above a banqueting scene.
Plain painted letters without serifs. Alpha is broad with straight bar; theta is nearly circular with horizontal bar; lunate sigma and lunate omega.

1Διόδωρος ἥ<ρ>ως ἀγ-
2αθός

l.1 ρ
1[---]spex

Neatly painted square letters with short serifs, painted in the style of stone cut letters. Alpha is broad with straight bar; Epsilon has equal length horizontal strokes; theta is ovoid with a full horizontal bar; kappa has arms that are not quite ful length; mu has vertical first and last, with middle strokes nearly reaching down to the line; pi has equal length verticals; sigma is four-bar, of equal length, top and bottom strokes horizontal.

1Ἀχιλλεὺς Προθύμου
2χαῖρε καὶ κατὰ
3χθονός
Four lines of Greek text, engraved in stucco and painted in red, across the full width of the rectangular block, with a vacat below.
Suggested to date to the later 3rd or earlier 2nd century BCE

4πλάγξασα ἐκ πάτρης τῆλε φίλ[ης] [ὄ]λεσεν
Incised and painted, one word per line within a recessed frame (H 14.5 cm x W 13.5 cm), stucco on stone.
